queries: protected path blocklist validation before write
autofix apply patch working tree host path
CLI flag definition and parsing autofix
gitdiff file OldMode NewMode executable bit

build_from.reason: applyOne is the single host-repo write choke point for --auto-fix: it already runs containedPath (a defense-in-depth path-containment check) immediately before branching into delete/modify/create. This is the exact integration point T2 wires pathguard.IsProtectedPath into, and T6's FlagsForReview into, since go-gitdiff's parsed File (f) already carries OldMode/NewMode at this point.
build_from.suggested_approach: Add a pathguard.IsProtectedPath(e.Path) check in applyOne right after containedPath succeeds and before refuseSymlinkLeaf/the delete-modify-create branches; thread an AllowConfigEdits bool into ApplyPatch's signature (or a package-level option struct) so cmd/atcr can pass the --allow-config-edits flag value through. For T6, call pathguard.FlagsForReview(e.Path, f.OldMode, f.NewMode) in the same place and collect flagged entries into a new return value alongside BackupMap.

existing_patterns.name: Denylist-style path validator returning typed ValidationError
existing_patterns.description: internal/validation/validation.go's FilePath function rejects paths under system directories (/etc, /proc, /sys plus macOS/Windows equivalents) with pure string/prefix matching, no I/O, returning a *ValidationError with Field/Value/Message. windowsSystemPath shows the pattern for a host-independent secondary check appended to the primary Unix check.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Shape/testing convention for internal/security/pathguard.go's IsProtectedPath — same no-I/O, prefix-boundary-matching, typed-error style, though pathguard blocks repo-relative config dirs (.git/, .github/, etc.) rather than absolute system dirs, so it stays a separate package per the epic's refinement notes.

existing_patterns.name: Package-var indirection for testability of a production primitive
existing_patterns.description: internal/autofix/apply.go uses removeFn/writeFileAtomicFn package vars so tests can drive failure branches deterministically without touching real I/O. cmd/atcr/autofix.go uses resolveHeadSHAFn/newAutoFixGitHubFn the same way for git/GitHub calls.
existing_patterns.follow_for: internal/gitexec's design: expose the wrapped exec.Command/exec.CommandContext constructor as a var-swappable function so the six migrated call sites (and their existing tests) can substitute a fake without spawning real git.

existing_patterns.name: Fail-closed CLI flag gating with an explicit opt-out
existing_patterns.description: cmd/atcr/autofix.go's --no-sandbox flag is the precedent for --allow-config-edits: off by default, requires an explicit true value to bypass a safety gate, and validateAutoFixBackend documents the risk in the flag's help text plus an unconditional (non-memoized) warning on every use (warnNoSandbox).
existing_patterns.follow_for: Registering --allow-config-edits in addAutoFixFlags with the same off-by-default, explicit-opt-out shape and a security-implication warning, then documenting it in docs/security.md per T4.

related_files.purpose: ApplyPatch/applyOne — the host-repo write path for --auto-fix; already has containedPath (path-containment defense-in-depth) as the single choke point before delete/modify/create branching.

Existing denylist-style path validator (FilePath) with no I/O, typed ValidationError, and a Windows-path secondary check — a structural precedent for pathguard.go, not something pathguard.go calls into.

CLI-level gate/orchestration for --auto-fix: addAutoFixFlags registers flags (T4's --allow-config-edits goes here), resolveHeadSHAFn is one of the six git subprocess call sites (line ~512), and runAutoFix's PR-body construction (near CreatePullRequest) is where T6's flagged-path warning section gets appended.

Two git subprocess call sites in one file: runGit (line 392, used by checkout/add/commit/push/clone) and gitHasStagedChanges (line 435, a direct exec.CommandContext not routed through runGit). Both must migrate to internal/gitexec for AC4.
git subprocess call site at line 1611 (exec.Command("git", "-C", repo, "rev-parse", ...)) — one of the six AC4 migration targets.
git subprocess call site at line 162 (exec.CommandContext(g.ctx, "git", full...)) — one of the six AC4 migration targets.
git subprocess call site at line 188 (exec.CommandContext(g.ctx, "git", full...)) — one of the six AC4 migration targets.
git subprocess call site at line 51 (exec.CommandContext(ctx, "git", "-C", root, "ls-files", "-z")) — one of the six AC4 migration targets.

Runs the operator-configured validate_command via exec.CommandContext(runCtx, argv[0], argv[1:]...) — argv[0] is whatever the project configures, not necessarily "git", so it is out of scope for the git-specific gitexec migration despite matching the exec.CommandContext grep.
Runs docker (b.cfg.DockerPath), not git, via exec.CommandContext — out of scope for gitexec despite matching the same grep pattern.

Generic atomic-write/backup primitives (WriteFileAtomic, BackupToDotBak) that internal/autofix/apply.go wraps. Confirmed NOT the right T2 integration point per refine-epic's finding — it has no concept of an --auto-fix write or AllowConfigEdits, and is shared by ~8 unrelated packages.

integration_points.description: Single choke point after containedPath succeeds, before the delete/modify/create branches. T2's pathguard.IsProtectedPath check and T6's FlagsForReview check both attach here.
Where --allow-config-edits is registered (T4), following the --no-sandbox off-by-default/explicit-opt-out precedent already in this function.
PR-body construction point where T6's visible warning section (naming flagged paths + reasons) gets appended when ApplyPatch's flagged-entries return value is non-empty.

integration_gaps.gap: Host write path blocklist enforcement before atomic write
integration_gaps.resolution: Wire pathguard.IsProtectedPath into applyOne choke point

integration_gaps.gap: Host git subprocess invocations lack environment hardening (GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM=1, GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null)
integration_gaps.affected_component: cmd/atcr/autofix.go, internal/personas/submit.go, internal/fanout/review.go, internal/gitrange/resolver.go, internal/payload/diff.go, internal/stream/fileindex.go
integration_gaps.resolution: Migrate all six git subprocess call sites to internal/gitexec wrapper

integration_gaps.gap: Executable-bit and build-script changes bypass visual review surfacing in PR body
integration_gaps.resolution: Implement non-blocking FlagsForReview and thread warning entries into PR body

reusable_components.name: validation.ValidationError
reusable_components.usage_example: Not reused directly (different path domain — repo-relative config dirs vs. absolute system dirs per the epic's refinement), but its Field/Value/Message shape and no-I/O testing convention should be mirrored in pathguard.go's own error type.

architecture_notes: internal/gitexec does not exist yet; it is a genuinely new package, not an extension of an existing helper — all six call sites currently construct exec.Command/exec.CommandContext independently with no shared env handling.

internal/atomicfs is a low-level, auto-fix-agnostic primitive shared by ~8 packages; gating logic must NOT go there (confirmed by refine-epic finding — would require a signature change threaded through every unrelated caller).
go-gitdiff's parsed File struct (from gitdiff.Parse in applyOne) already exposes OldMode/NewMode fields needed for T6's executable-bit detection — no additional parsing step required.
--auto-fix never auto-merges: runAutoFix always ends at gh.CreatePullRequest/UpdatePullRequest, never a merge call, confirming human review is already the terminal gate T6's warning surfaces into.

files_to_modify.reason: Wire pathguard.IsProtectedPath (T2) and pathguard.FlagsForReview (T6) into applyOne's single choke point; thread an AllowConfigEdits option and a flagged-entries return value out of ApplyPatch.
Register --allow-config-edits (T4); migrate resolveHeadSHAFn's git subprocess call to internal/gitexec (T3); append T6's flagged-path warning section to the PR body near CreatePullRequest.
Migrate the git subprocess call at line 1611 to internal/gitexec (T3/AC4).
Migrate the git subprocess call at line 162 to internal/gitexec (T3/AC4).
Migrate the git subprocess call at line 188 to internal/gitexec (T3/AC4).
Migrate both git subprocess call sites (runGit at line 392, gitHasStagedChanges at line 435) to internal/gitexec (T3/AC4).
Migrate the git subprocess call at line 51 to internal/gitexec (T3/AC4).
Add an index entry for the new docs/security.md so it is linked in the canonical doc list the website build consumes (T4).

files_to_create.purpose: IsProtectedPath(path string) bool blocklist check (T1) plus the non-blocking FlagsForReview(path string, oldMode, newMode int) (bool, reason string) check (T6).
files_to_create.based_on: internal/validation/validation.go (structural precedent — denylist matching, typed error, no I/O)

files_to_create.purpose: Unit tests for IsProtectedPath and FlagsForReview covering canonical, relative, and symlink-traversal path formats (T5/AC3).
Shared wrapper around exec.Command/exec.CommandContext for git invocations, injecting GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM=1, GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null, and --no-ext-diff where applicable (T3).
files_to_create.based_on: internal/autofix/apply.go's removeFn/writeFileAtomicFn package-var indirection pattern, for testability

files_to_create.purpose: Unit tests for the gitexec wrapper plus regression coverage confirming each of the six migrated call sites invokes git through internal/gitexec rather than a bare exec.Command (T5/AC4).

test_patterns.example_test: internal/autofix/apply_test.go (not read in this pass, but the package convention per internal/autofix/apply.go's doc comments)
